title: Scale dashboards with data source variables

blocks.content:
Welcome to the Grafana learning path that walks you through creating and using data source variables to build scalable dashboards.

As your infrastructure grows, you might add Prometheus servers per region, PostgreSQL databases per team, or monitoring backends per environment. Instead of duplicating queries for each instance, data source variables let users select which instance to query from a dropdown at the top of the dashboard.

## Here's what to expect

When you complete this path, you'll be able to:

- Create a data source variable that lists all instances of a specific data source type
- Use the variable in a panel so the query targets whichever instance the user selects
- Enable multi-value selection so users can query multiple instances at once

## Before you begin

Before you create a data source variable, ensure you have:

- A Grafana Cloud account. To create one, refer to [Grafana Cloud](https://grafana.com/signup/cloud/connect-account).
- An existing dashboard (or the ability to create a new one).
- At least one configured data source. This path uses the built-in Grafana (TestData) data source, so no external configuration is required.

## Troubleshooting

If you get stuck, we've got your back! Where appropriate, troubleshooting information is just a click away.

## More to explore

We understand you might want to explore other capabilities not strictly on this path. We'll provide you opportunities where it makes sense.
